{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe Burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli form a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped forms.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | Burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | Burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe Burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | Period = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == Structure and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:dd]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:dd-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}} [[Category:New]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are other_designation1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing addition[[Category:addition]]al haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:addition]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Saki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Konabe Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:dd-1月4日 (旧暦)-dd]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Konabe Kofun}}

It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|12=draft}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=draft}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|12=draft}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路|12=draft}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} | location = {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|ja|5世紀|12=draft}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) {{ill|Princess Iwanohime|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa|12=draft}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=draft}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|12=draft}} | image = [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]]
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōen-fun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=draft}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|ja|磐之媛命|1=Princess Iwa|12=draft}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=draft}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|ja|航空自衛隊|12=draft}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=draft}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|ja|平城宮|12=draft}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=draft}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|12=draft}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=draft}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=draft}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|JR Nara Station|ja|奈良駅|12=draft}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|ja|近鉄奈良駅|12=draft}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|ja|第二阪奈道路|12=draft}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Imperial Tomb Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute.
