The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪}} | image =
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:佐保佐紀路]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 41 | coordinates = 51.69 | coordinates = 135 | coordinates = 48 | coordinates = 16.98 | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪}} | image =
In the photo: three zenpō-kōen-fun tumuli. The central mound is '''Konabe Kofun'''. To the right (east) is {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, and to the left (northwest) is {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}}. | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniba Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniba Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniba Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=representational haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Konabe Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[en:Konabe Kofun]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11302279]] Wikidata name: Konabe Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489}} | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.697692|N|135.804717|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=representational haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[sn:Konabe Kofun]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[en:Konabe Kofun]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}

Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=representational haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}

In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}

Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=representational haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}

Height 20 meters | built = Early [[:en:5th century|5th century]] | burials = (Proposed by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]] | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=representational haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as a "burial reference site" for [[:en:Princess Iwa|Princess Iwa]], consort of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara City]]. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the [[:en:Japan Air Self-Defense Force|Japan Air Self-Defense Force]] Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at [[:en:Heijō Palace|Heijō Palace]]. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the [[:en:The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|Kashihara Archaeological Institute]] confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}

In 2009, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From [[:en:Nara Station|JR Nara Station]] or [[:en:Kintetsu Nara Station|Kintetsu Nara Station]], take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the [[:en:Daini Hanna Road|Daini Hanna Road]], follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}}
