In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|WD=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.697692|N|135.804717|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|WD=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|WD=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|WD=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|WD=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|WD=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|WD=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|WD=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|WD=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|WD=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|WD=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}} [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|qid=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}} [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.697692|N|135.804717|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|qid=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|qid=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|qid=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}} [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Konabe Kofun]][[ja:コナベ古墳]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Konabe Kofun | Alternative name = 小奈辺古墳 | part_of = {{ill|Saki-Tatenami Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11383489|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group}} | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.697692|N|135.804717|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-29 | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | area = Length 204 meters
Height 20 meters | built = Early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | burials = (Proposed by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}}) {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}} | burial Management = Imperial Household Agency "Konabe burial Reference Site" | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|House-shaped haniwa|ja|家形埴輪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/House-shaped haniwa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487889}} | image = File:Konabe-kofun.jpg | notes = Ranked 34th-largest tumulus in Japan[https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/dkofun/Ranking/zenkoku.html National Kofun area Rankings], Sakai City website, updated May 13, 2018. | map = Nara city | map_dot_label = Konabe Kofun | type = Zenpō-kōen-fun-南 | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} == Overview == The '''Konabe Kofun''' (小奈辺古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} (keyhole-shaped tumulus) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Saki Kofun Cluster|ja|佐紀盾列古墳群|1=Saki Tatenami Kofun group|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Kofun Cluster|13=User:Immanuelle/Saki Tatenami Kofun group|qq=}} and is managed by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as a "burial reference site" for {{ill|Princess Iwa|en|Princess Iwa|qid=Q1066593|lt=Princess Iwa|1=Princess Iwa}}, consort of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}. == burial and location == [[File:CG Konabe 2 7pbw.jpg|thumb|260px|right|3D rendering of Konabe Kofun]] Konabe Kofun is located on the southwestern slopes of the Saki Hills in northern {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara City|1=Nara (city)}}. It belongs to the eastern group of the Saki Kofun Cluster, situated between the {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishage Kofun|lt=Hishiage Kofun|ja|ヒシアゲ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hishiage Kofun|qq=}} to the northwest. These three tumuli type a triangle surrounding the {{ill|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|en|Japan Air Self-Defense Force|qid=Q830305|lt=Japan Air Self-Defense Force|1=Japan Air Self-Defense Force}} Officer Candidate School and are located about 750 meters northeast of the {{ill|Daigokuden|ja|大極殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daigokuden|qq=}} site at {{ill|Heijō Palace|en|Heijō Palace|qid=Q211585|lt=Heijō Palace|1=Heijō Palace}}. The tumulus is 204 meters in length, Ranking as the 34th-largest in Japan. It features a three-tiered construction with the following area: * Rear circular section diameter: 125 meters, height: 20 meters * Front section width: 129 meters, height: 17.5 meters * Orientation: Southward The tumulus shares many characteristics with its counterpart, {{ill|Uwanabe Kofun|ja|ウワナベ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uwanabe Kofun|qq=}}, but is believed to have been built slightly earlier. The type and construction techniques of Konabe Kofun have been compared to those of the {{ill|Ichiniwa Kofun|ja|市庭古墳|1=Ichiniwa Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiniwa Kofun|qq=}} in Nara and the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} in Sakai, Osaka. File:CG Konabe 1 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the east File:CG Konabe 3 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view of the front section File:CG Konabe 6 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the southwest File:CG Konabe 7 7pbw.jpg|3D rendering, view from the northwest File:CG Konabe 8 7p.jpg|3D rendering, side view == burial goods and Research == Excavations of the Konabe Kofun have identified haniwa rows along its three-tiered structure. Cylindrical haniwa, as well as house-shaped and fence-shaped haniwa, have been unearthed. The mound was also adorned with fukiishi (cover stones) of varying sizes. * In 1979, a survey by the {{ill|Nara City Board of Education|ja|奈良市教育委員会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara City Board of Education|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} uncovered haniwa rows along the southern edge of the front section.Imao (1996), p. 300-301 * In 1997, the {{ill|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|en|The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture|qid=Q112961122|lt=Kashihara Archaeological Institute|1=The Museum, Archaeological Institute of Kashihara, Nara Prefecture}} confirmed the presence of an outer moat and excavated triangular iron plate armor fragments and iron arrowheads. In 2009, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} conducted an excavation revealing additional haniwa fragments, including cylindrical and house-shaped types.[http://osaka.yomiuri.co.jp/inishie/news/20091205-OYO8T00325.htm "Konabe Kofun Reference Site Opened to Media"], Yomiuri Shimbun, December 5, 2009. == Associated Kofuns == Ten subsidiary tumuli (陪塚) are located along the moat surrounding the main tumulus. These smaller mounds range in size from dd to 42 meters and include both square and round types. Seven of these mounds are Historic Site1 as part of the burial reference site. == location and Access == ;location * Konabe Kofun, Hōkeshichō, Nara City, Nara Prefecture, Japan ;Access * By train: From {{ill|Nara Station|en|Nara Station|qid=Q1150978|lt=JR Nara Station|1=Nara Station}} or {{ill|Kintetsu Nara Station|en|Kintetsu Nara Station|qid=Q6414371|lt=Kintetsu Nara Station|1=Kintetsu Nara Station}}, take a bus to "Jieitai-mae" and alight at the final stop. * By car: From the {{ill|Hōrai Interchange|ja|宝来ランプ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōrai Interchange|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宝来インターチェンジ (奈良県)|1=Horai interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452573}} on the {{ill|Daini Hanna Road|en|Daini Hanna Road|qid=Q11602338|lt=Daini Hanna Road|1=Daini Hanna Road}}, follow National Routes 308 and 24. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Otsuka, Hajime, and Kobayashi, Saburō. ''Kofun Jiten.'' Tōkyōdō Shuppan, 1982. ISBN 4-490-10165-1. * Imao, Fumiaki. "Explanation of Mausoleum Kofun." In Mori, Hiroshi (Ed.). ''Tennō-ryō Kofun.'' Daikōsha, 1996. ISBN 4-924899-09-7. * Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. ''Excavation Reports on Yamato,'' 2009. == External Links == * [http://www.oct.zaq.ne.jp/kashiko_yushikai/reikai/reikai0602.htm Walking the Saki Kofun Cluster], Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute. {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|コナベ古墳|version=103945976|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[image:Sakitatanamikofungun.jpg|300px]] [[ja:コナベ古墳]] {{Commons category|Konabe Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11302279}} [[Category:Historic Saho-Saki Road]] [[Category:History of Nara]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Saki Tatenami Kofun group]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
